title: Analyze JSON Logs With jq
description:
## Count Errors in Structured Logs

Structured logging emits one JSON object per line. A file **`/root/app.log`** contains such lines, each with a **`level`** field.

**Your task:** using **`jq`**, count how many lines have **`level == "error"`** and write just the number to **`/root/errors.txt`**.

> 💡 `jq` reads a stream of JSON values. Use `-s` (slurp) to collect them into an array, filter with `select(.level == "error")`, and take the `length`.

hints.title: Slurp, filter, count
hints.body: Slurp all lines into an array, keep only error-level entries, then count them.

validation.description: Checks that /root/errors.txt contains the count of error-level lines (2).
